mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a sharp contraction in economic activity and falling consumer demand, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns as recession risk is priced in.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has risen from a low of 4.4% in early 2007 to 5.0% by Dec 2007
failure_condition: Triggered instances are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts indicates weakening consumer confidence and residential investment, which precedes broader economic slowdowns and negatively impacts corporate profits and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST has fallen from over 2000k units in 2006 to under 1200k by late 2007
failure_condition: The 12-month change in HOUST has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 20-day forward returns that is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is actively deteriorating, Federal Reserve rate cuts are interpreted by the market not as stimulus, but as a confirmation of severe underlying economic weakness, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has cut rates while unemployment is rising
failure_condition: When UNRATE 6-month change is positive, periods of falling FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production growth reflects falling aggregate demand and inventory adjustments, signaling lower near-term corporate revenue growth and pressuring equity prices downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO year-over-year growth has slowed significantly through 2007
failure_condition: The 6-month change in INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive rank correlation with 60-day forward returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation exceeds a critical threshold, it erodes corporate profit margins via higher input costs and reduces the real value of future earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI YoY was over 4% in late 2007
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically lower on average when YoY CPI is above 4.0% compared to when it is below.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a significant rise in the unemployment rate from its recent low and decelerating payroll growth provides a robust, real-time signal of a recession, causing a sharp repricing of equity risk.
failure_condition: Instances where the trigger condition is met are not followed by negative 60-day returns in at least 65% of cases over the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates an immediate weakening of consumer spending, which constitutes a large portion of GDP, leading to negative revisions in earnings forecasts and short-term negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales showed significant weakness in late 2007
failure_condition: The information coefficient of month-over-month RSAFS changes on 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently negative over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive monetary easing, proxied by a sharp decline in the effective federal funds rate, lowers the discount rate for future cash flows and signals policy support for the economy, boosting equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has cut rates from 5.25% to 4.24% since mid-2007
failure_condition: Periods following a >75bp cut in FEDFUNDS over 3 months do not exhibit statistically significant positive abnormal returns compared to other periods.
